title: Rules and Regulations
content: Rules and Regulations Rules and Regulations of Competitions conducted by the society : Before you attempt programming questions, you should choose a language from the given list. All inputs for the programming problems are from STDIN and output to STDOUT. To understand more about the judging environment, time limits, etc. you can read information about the HackerEarth judge . Programming questions have a Compile & Run option which you can use to run your solution against sample test cases before submitting it. You can submit the solution for a programming problem 300 times per day. For subjective and objective problems, you can change your answer as many times as you want. If any plagiarism is found the candidate will be disqualified automatically by the judging environment. The ranking will be done be judge environment itself and leader board will be shared by the society once competition is over. In case of discrepancy the judgement of the Coding Society JUIT would be final.
